report_name: Supply Chain Attacks Hit 26% of UK Businesses in the Last Year

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Attackers abuse the trusted relationship between an organization and its suppliers to gain access to other networks, which is the core of a supply chain attack.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Attackers compromise a single, often less-secure, provider in a supply chain to affect multiple targets. This can involve compromising software, hardware, or managed services.

identities.name: UK Businesses
identities.description: A class representing businesses in the United Kingdom, 26% of which were reported to have suffered a supply chain cyber incident in the past year according to a Databarracks survey.

identities.name: Databarracks
identities.description: Business continuity firm that authored the 'Data Health Check 2026' report on supply chain attacks.

indicators.name: Suspicious Remote Management Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Remote Management Tool Execution
id: 9b8c6d5a-1f2e-4b3c-8a7b-9d8e7f6a5b4c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of common remote management tools like TeamViewer or AnyDesk, which can be abused by attackers in supply chain compromises, as mentioned in the report's cyber observables section.
references:
  - https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/21/1-in-4-businesses-hit-by-cyber-attacks-through-their-supply-chain-in-the-last-year/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-21
tags:
  - attack.t1219
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\TeamViewer.exe'
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of remote management tools by IT administrators or support staff.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of common remote management tools (e.g., TeamViewer, AnyDesk) that can be abused by attackers in supply chain compromises.
